You invite your friend Alex to attend a lecture on negotiation. Imagine that. The speaker, a former FBI Haas's negotiator, asks who among the attendees has a birthday closest to the date of the lecture. As it turns out, it's Alex. He then asks Alex if he is in attendance with anyone and he identifies you. This mysterious FBI speaker states he is so impressed that the two of you are here together that he'd like to offer you both a share of $10. But only if Alex makes a single written offer to you on how to split it. Before seeking Alex's written offer, you have to write down the amount you're willing to accept. If Alex's offer to you matches or exceeds the amount you're willing to accept, it's split as Alex has proposed. If Alex offers you less than what you're willing to accept, no one gets anything. What do you write down that you're willing to accept?
